qtype: Integer operations with negative numbers, temperature change
tex:
(a) The temperature on Monday was -7 °C.
\newline
The temperature on Tuesday was 5 °C lower than on Monday.
\newline
The temperature on Wednesday was 8 °C higher than on Tuesday.
\newline
Find the temperature on Wednesday.
\newline

 °C

(b) Kyra has a faulty thermometer.
\newline
It always shows the temperature as 2 °C higher than the actual temperature.
\newline
The temperature on the thermometer is T °C.
\newline
Write an expression, in terms of T, for the actual temperature.
\newline

 °C

content.content:
The temperature on Monday was -7 °C.
\newline
The temperature on Tuesday was 5 °C lower than on Monday.
\newline
The temperature on Wednesday was 8 °C higher than on Tuesday.
\newline
Find the temperature on Wednesday.
\newline

Kyra has a faulty thermometer.
\newline
It always shows the temperature as 2 °C higher than the actual temperature.
\newline
The temperature on the thermometer is T °C.
\newline
Write an expression, in terms of T, for the actual temperature.
\newline

_rawBody:
\begin{parts}
\item The temperature on Monday was -7 °C.
The temperature on Tuesday was 5 °C lower than on Monday.
The temperature on Wednesday was 8 °C higher than on Tuesday.
Find the temperature on Wednesday.

\AnswerLine[] °C
\Marks{2}

\item Kyra has a faulty thermometer.
It always shows the temperature as 2 °C higher than the actual temperature.
The temperature on the thermometer is T °C.
Write an expression, in terms of T, for the actual temperature.

\AnswerLine[] °C
\Marks{1}

\end{parts}
